questions.prompt:
Your professor is teaching a class. Write a post
responding to the professor's question.
In , you should do the following.
* Express and support your personal opinion;
* Make a contribution to the discussion in your
own words;
An effective response will contain at least 100
words.
Dr. Diaz
Today, we're revisiting the topic of corporate
transparency, which refers to how openly
companies share internal information with their
employees. Proponents argue it builds trust and
‘engagement, while others believe some
information should remain confidential to maintain
competitive edges or protect sensitive data.
Balancing openness and protection is a real-world
challenge for many organizations. So, do you think
corporate transparency is essential, or should
some information be kept confidential? Why?
Paul
Claire
I believe conformity helps maintain social harmony. When individuals
conform to societal norms, it should reduce conflict and foster
cooperation. This in turn leads to a more stable and cohesive
community. I see this in my neighborhood, where everyone helps each
other out.
I think conformity suppresses individuality. By always trying to fit in with
the group, people may avoid expressing their own perspectives. This
seems to happen in social media quite often, where posts often just
sound like repetitions or echoes of others.
kelly
Paul
J
09:30 @ Hide Time
believe corporate transparency is essential because it builds trust and
motivates employees. When people understand company goals and
financials, they feel more involved and committed. For example,
startups often share everything, which encourages innovation and
loyalty among team members.
I think some information should be kept confidential. Revealing sensitive
data, like future product plans or financial struggles, could harm a
company's position. Competitors might take advantage, and employees
might panic. Full transparency can be risky in highly competitive
industries.(based on real test)
